mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market exceeding a 0.4 percentage point increase over three months signals an imminent economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates genuine real demand expansion without monetary tightening pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative predictive value below 0.40 over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or SPX forward returns show no statistical difference from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rebounds above its 12-month moving average during a post-shock recovery phase, confirming supply normalization and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns do not exceed the unconditional mean by at least 0.5% during post-shock recovery quarters, or the signal's hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in retail sales and food services exceeding 8 percent reflects a sudden demand shock that erodes corporate revenue expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines exceeding 8% do not precede negative 20-day equity returns in more than 60% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous elevated federal funds rates and rising unemployment create a stagflationary environment that forces equity valuation compression, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns across a 60-day window.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve model R-squared by at least 0.02 over a linear baseline, or forward returns remain positive during stagflationary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth consistently exceeding the historical trend rate validates corporate profit expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the immediate 5 trading day horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth above trend does not correlate with positive 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.05) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls accelerating above 200,000 monthly adds during an early-cycle expansion phase confirms sustainable labor demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Monthly payroll additions above 200k during early-cycle phases fail to generate positive 20-day forward returns in at least 60% of observations.
